overview: Simic Scapeshift leverages landfall synergies and massive mana acceleration to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. By cycling lands into the graveyard and replaying them with Scapeshift, the deck generates explosive board states and outscales most Pioneer strategies. Success relies on carefully sequencing land drops to maximize triggers while maintaining enough resources to cast high-impact threats.
win_conditions: Casting high-mana-cost threats that close the game
Flooding the board with mana-producing lands to outpace opponent's removal

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and a ramp spell
Scapeshift paired with early landfall triggers
Spelunking to fix mana and set up the graveyard
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches/mana sources), Spelunking, Lumra, or other ramp spells.
Prioritize hands that can reliably hit 3, 4, and 5 mana on curve.
mulligan_criteria: Zero lands or only high-cost cards
Hands lacking early interaction or acceleration
Mulligan pure curve decks, hands with only 1 land, or hands featuring Scapeshift without sufficient ramp or card draw to support it.

early_game: Play lands consistently to build a base
Use Spelunking to fetch specific land types and sacrifice lands to the graveyard
Deploy early landfall creatures to apply pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Spelunking or a ramp spell.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Lumra or another acceleration piece.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your spells on curve
use fetches to find duals or shock lands as needed.
Turn 3: Play a land, cast a second ramp or draw spell.
mid_game: Cast Scapeshift to replay lands from the graveyard
Chain landfall triggers to deploy threats and generate value
Manage life total carefully when using life-drain ramp
Prioritize keeping lands in play and in the graveyard for Scapeshift targets.
Use Lumra’s card draw to dig for Scapeshift or finishers rather than overcommitting to creatures.
Manage your graveyard carefully if hate is present, and avoid casting Scapeshift if you don’t have enough lands to sacrifice for a meaningful board state.
Commit threats only when you have a clear path to going off or can stabilize against aggression.
Hold back Scapeshift if the opponent has significant removal, counters, or hate in play.
Use counterspells to protect key ramp pieces or the Scapeshift itself, and only attack with creatures when you can safely close the game or force a favorable trade.
late_game: Flood the board with additional lands to produce massive mana
Cast win-condition threats that scale with land count
Use excess mana to cast multiple spells or activate abilities
Scapeshift into Forest's Caress, Tron, or a massive creature/planeswalker.
Sometimes just going wide with basic lands and a finisher.
When you have 7+ lands in play/graveyard, a clear path to cast Scapeshift, and the opponent has no immediate answer.
Also commit if you're behind on board and need to go off to win.

key_cards.name: Scapeshift
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have lands in the graveyard to trigger landfall and accelerate mana production.

key_cards.name: Lumra, Bellow of the Woods
key_cards.notes: Enters with a landfall trigger and untaps two lands, enabling faster Scapeshift casts.

key_cards.name: Spelunking
key_cards.notes: Search for needed lands and put others into the graveyard to fuel Scapeshift, but watch your life total.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize putting lands into the graveyard before casting Scapeshift
Trigger landfall abilities in an order that maximizes board impact
Save Spelunking for when you need specific land types or graveyard fuel

Common mistakes: Going off too early without a finisher, overcommitting to creatures, forgetting that Scapeshift sacrifices lands (so you need enough in play/graveyard), miscounting landfall triggers.
Unique interactions: Lumra's landfall can trigger multiple times if you fetch/Scapeshift.
Spelunking can find basic lands for Scapeshift targets.

matchups.advice: Ramp quickly to stabilize and use landfall triggers to outvalue their early pressure.
Race their hand disruption by flooding the board with lands and casting threats they cannot answer.
Outpace their curve by accelerating mana and deploying multiple threats per turn.
Use multiple cheap threats to force counters, cast Scapeshift in response to a counter, or use counterspells to protect your own spells. Sequence carefully.
Race them or disrupt their mana. Use counterspells on key ramp spells. Scapeshift faster.
It's a resource race. Win the card advantage war, protect your Scapeshift, and use counterspells to protect your own finishers. Track graveyard hate.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of Punishment), counterspells, and board wipes.
Note if the opponent is playing fast aggro (track life total and board presence) or control (track removal count and available counters).
Pay attention to when they tap out, as that’s your window to go off.
